Package org.rodinp.internal.core

Class RodinFile

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject
org.rodinp.core.basis.RodinElement
org.rodinp.internal.core.Openable
org.rodinp.internal.core.RodinFile
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IOpenable, IParent, IRodinElement, IRodinFile, ISnapshotable
public final class RodinFile extends Openable implements IRodinFile
Represents an entire Rodin file. File elements need to be opened before they can be navigated or manipulated.
See Also:
  • IRodinFile

  • Constructor Details

    • RodinFile

      public RodinFile(org.eclipse.core.resources.IFile file, IRodinElement parent)

  • Method Details

    • findElements

      @Deprecated public final IRodinElement[] findElements(IRodinElement element)
      Deprecated.
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Finds the elements in this file that correspond to the given element. An element A corresponds to an element B if:
      • A has the same element type and name as B.
      • The parent of A corresponds to the parent of B recursively up to their respective files.
      • A exists.
      Returns null if no such Rodin elements can be found or if the given element is not included in a file.
      Specified by:
      findElements in interface IRodinFile
      Parameters:
      element - the given element
      Returns:
      the found elements in this file that correspond to the given element

    • clear

      public void clear(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Removes all attributes and children of this element, forcing if specified and necessary.
      Specified by:
      clear in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      force - a flag controlling whether underlying resources that are not in sync with the local file system will be tolerated (same as the force flag in IResource operations).
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be cleared. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource (CORE_EXCEPTION)
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)

    • close

      public void close() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Closes this element and unloads it. Closing an element which is not open has no effect.

      Note: although IOpenable.close() is exposed in the API, clients are not expected to open and close elements - the Rodin database does this automatically as elements are accessed.

      Specified by:
      close in interface IOpenable
      Overrides:
      close in class Openable
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs closing this element

    • closing

      public void closing(OpenableElementInfo info)
      Description copied from class: Openable
      This element is being closed. Do any necessary cleanup.
      Overrides:
      closing in class Openable

    • copy

      public final void copy(IRodinElement container, IRodinElement sibling, String rename, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Copies this element to the given container.
      Specified by:
      copy in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      container - the destination container
      sibling - the sibling element before which the copy should be inserted, or null if the copy should be inserted as the last child of the container
      rename - the new name for the element, or null if the copy retains the name of this element
      replace - true if any existing child in the container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be copied. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element, container element, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • The sibling is not a child of the given container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • The container or this element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • The destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • create

      public void create(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Creates this file in the database. As a side effect, all ancestors of this element are open if they were not already.

      It is possible that this file already exists. The value of the force parameter effects the resolution of such a conflict:

      • true - in this case the file is created anew with empty contents
      • false - in this case a RodinDBException is thrown

      Specified by:
      create in interface IRodinFile
      Parameters:
      force - specify how to handle conflict is this element already exists
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element could not be created. Reasons include:
      • The parent of this element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This file element exists and force is false (NAME_COLLISION)
      • A CoreException occurred while creating an underlying resource

    • delete

      public final void delete(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Deletes this element, forcing if specified and necessary.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      force - a flag controlling whether underlying resources that are not in sync with the local file system will be tolerated (same as the force flag in IResource operations).
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be deleted. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource (CORE_EXCEPTION)
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Description copied from class: RodinElement
      Returns true if this handle represents the same Rodin element as the given handle. By default, two handles represent the same element if they are identical or if they represent the same type of element, have equal names and parents.

      If a subclass has other requirements for equality, this method must be overridden.

      Overrides:
      equals in class RodinElement
      See Also:
      • Object.equals(java.lang.Object)

    • exists

      public boolean exists()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element exists in the database.

      Rodin elements are handle objects that may or may not be backed by an actual element. Rodin elements that are backed by an actual element are said to "exist", and this method returns true. For Rodin elements, it is always the case that if the element exists, then its parent also exists (provided it has one). Moreover, for any element which is not a stable file snapshot, the parent includes the element as one of its children. It is therefore possible to navigate to any existing Rodin element (except stable file snapshots) from the root of the Rodin database along a chain of existing Rodin elements. On the other hand, a stable snapshot file never shows up among the children of its parent element.

      The fact that an openable element exists gives no guarantee upon whether it can be opened. For instance, a malformed Rodin file may exist, while it can never be opened by the database (because it contains invalid XML).

      A call to this method might open the ancestors of this element, but will never open this element itself.

      Specified by:
      exists in interface IRodinElement
      Overrides:
      exists in class Openable
      Returns:
      true if this element exists in the Rodin database, and false if this element does not exist

    • getBareName

      public String getBareName()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Returns the bare name of this Rodin file. The bare name of a Rodin file is its element name with the possible file extension removed.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getBareName in interface IRodinFile
      Returns:
      the bare name of this Rodin file
      See Also:
      • IResource.getFileExtension()

    • getPath

      public final org.eclipse.core.runtime.IPath getPath()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element. The path returned is the full, absolute path to the underlying resource, relative to the workspace.

      This is a handle-only method.

      This is a convenience method fully equivalent to getOpenable().getFullPath().

      Specified by:
      getPath in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element

      • one of its descendants has unsaved changes
      Specified by:

    • isConsistent

      Description copied from class: Openable
      Subclasses must override as required.
      Specified by:
      isConsistent in interface IOpenable
      Overrides:
      isConsistent in class Openable
      Returns:
      true if the element is consistent with its underlying resource, false otherwise.

      • IOpenable

    • isReadOnly

      public boolean isReadOnly()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only. An element can be read-only for two reasons: either it corresponds to a resource which is read-only, or it is an internal element that belongs to a stable snapshot of a Rodin file, or the stable snapshot itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      isReadOnly in interface IRodinElement
      Overrides:
      isReadOnly in class Openable
      Returns:
      true iff this element is read-only

    • isSnapshot

      public final boolean isSnapshot()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns whether this is a handle in a file snapshot.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      isSnapshot in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      true iff the corresponding element is or belongs to the stable snapshot of a Rodin file

    • makeConsistent

      public void makeConsistent(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Makes this element consistent with its underlying resource by updating the element's structure and attributes as necessary.
      Specified by:
      makeConsistent in interface IOpenable
      Overrides:
      makeConsistent in class Openable
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is unable to access the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      See Also:
      • IOpenable

    • move

      public final void move(IRodinElement container, IRodinElement sibling, String rename, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Moves this element to the given container.
      Specified by:
      move in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      container - the destination container
      sibling - the sibling element before which the element should be inserted, or null if the element should be inserted as the last child of the container
      rename - the new name for the element, or null if the element retains its name
      replace - true if any existing child in the container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be moved. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element, container element, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • The sibling is not a child of the given container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • The container or this element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element or the destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • rename

      public final void rename(String name, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Renames this element to the given name.
      Specified by:
      rename in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      name - the new name for the element
      replace - true if any existing element with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be renamed. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • revert

      public final void revert() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Reverts this file. Reverting a file has the effect of forgetting any unsaved change and closing the file.
      Specified by:
      revert in interface IRodinFile
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs closing this element

    • save

      public final void save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor, boolean force) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Saves any changes in this element to its underlying resource via a workspace resource operation. This has no effect if it has no unsaved changed.

      The force parameter controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system. If false is specified, this method will only attempt to overwrite a corresponding file in the local file system provided it is in sync with the workbench. This option ensures there is no unintended data loss; it is the recommended setting. However, if true is specified, an attempt will be made to write a corresponding file in the local file system, overwriting any existing one if need be. In either case, if this method succeeds, the resource will be marked as being local (even if it wasn't before).

      As a result of this operation, the element is consistent with its underlying resource.

      Specified by:
      save in interface IOpenable
      Overrides:
      save in class Openable
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      force - it controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This Rodin element is read-only (READ_ONLY)

    • save

      public final void save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor, boolean force, boolean keepHistory) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IOpenable
      Saves any changes in this element to its underlying resource via a workspace resource operation. This has no effect if it has no unsaved changed.

      The force parameter controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system. If false is specified, this method will only attempt to overwrite a corresponding file in the local file system provided it is in sync with the workbench. This option ensures there is no unintended data loss; it is the recommended setting. However, if true is specified, an attempt will be made to write a corresponding file in the local file system, overwriting any existing one if need be. In either case, if this method succeeds, the resource will be marked as being local (even if it wasn't before).

      As a result of this operation, the element is consistent with its underlying resource.

      Specified by:
      save in interface IOpenable
      Overrides:
      save in class Openable
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      force - it controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system
      keepHistory - a flag indicating whether or not to store the current contents in the local history
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This Rodin element is read-only (READ_ONLY)

    • getRoot

      public IInternalElement getRoot()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Returns the root internal element of this file.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getRoot in interface IRodinFile
      Returns:
      the root element of this Rodin file

      public IInternalElementType<?> getRootElementType()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinFile
      Returns the element type of the root element of this file.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getRootElementType in interface IRodinFile
      Returns:
      the type of the root element of this Rodin file
